21.kj: ask, beg, beseech, desire, enquire, get, make inquisition, procure, (make) request, require, seek (for).

21.gl: strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity)
21.kj: God (god), [idiom] goodly, [idiom] great, idol, might(-y one), power, strong. Compare names in '-el.'

21.gl: a village (as covered in by walls); also a young lion (perhaps as covered with a mane)
something torn, i.e. a fragment, e.g. a fresh leaf, prey, food

21.kj: eating, food, meal(-time), meat, prey, victuals.

22.gl: to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. remove (destroy, leave behind, put up, restore, etc.)

22.gl: the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. (architectural) a notched battlement
22.kj: [phrase] east side(-ward), sun (rising), [phrase] west(-ward), window. See also H1053 (בֵּית שֶׁמֶשׁ).
22.dv: from an unused root meaning to be brilliant;

22.gl: to crouch (on all four legs folded, like a recumbent animal); be implication, to recline, repose, brood, lurk, imbed
22.kj: crouch (down), fall down, make a fold, lay, (cause to, make to) lie (down), make to rest, sit.

22.gl: {an abode, of God (the Tabernacle or the Temple), men (their home) or animals (their lair); hence, a retreat (asylum)}

22.gl: properly, to irradiate (or shoot forth beams), i.e. to rise (as the sun); specifically, to appear (as a symptom of leprosy)
22.kj: arise, rise (up), as soon as it is up.

act, bondage, [phrase] bondservant, effect, labour, ministering(-try), office, service(-ile, -itude), tillage, use, work, [idiom] wrought.
act, deed, do, getting, maker, work.
